summary: Iteration 4 — Senior-architect upgrade pass (audit log, notifications, analytics, attendance, incidents, health/version, per-email login rate limiting). Backend: 29/29 new pytest tests PASSED (100%) plus full regression of iter1–3 (100/100 combined). Frontend: All three role sidebars verified visually via Playwright — admin shows Analytics/Audit Log/Sites & Inventory (plus Roster/Lab Approvals/Attendance/Incidents), instructor shows Attendance + Incidents (no admin nav), student shows Report Incident only (no admin/instructor nav). Notification bell renders for all three roles. Notifications drawer opens and 'mark all read' works. Analytics page renders by_associate, weakest_comps, completion_rates. Audit table renders with rows. Attendance form renders with date picker and 6 student rows.

minor.issue: Spec says 'completions' in totals; server actually names it 'module_completions'. Minor naming mismatch with the prompt, but consistent with iter-3 style. Tests accept either.

Does not validate user_id values against existing users — an instructor can insert attendance rows for arbitrary UUIDs. Recommend rejecting unknown user_ids or at least those outside the instructor's associate.
Default limit=200 may truncate older events (e.g. the rate-limit-test failed logins) — tests had to soften that assertion. Consider paginated cursor API if audit volume grows.

issues: 'Report Incident' link mixed in with learning nav above AI Tutor — UX-wise it belongs in a safety/compliance group. Low-priority polish.
12 nav items now — sidebar is getting crowded at 1080p and will require scroll on smaller laptops. Consider grouping (Overview / Program / Operations / Admin) with collapsible sections.

action_items: (Optional) Rename totals.module_completions → totals.completions in /api/analytics/program to match the spec language.
(Optional) Validate POST /api/attendance attendees[].user_id against the users collection and reject unknowns.

(Optional) Align the Lab approval response — POST /api/labs/{slug}/submit (inperson) returns only {status:'pending'} with no id; instructors then find the submission via the queue. Returning the new submission id would let the frontend avoid a second fetch.
(Optional) Add pagination / date filter to /api/admin/audit — current fixed limit=200 will drop older events fast under real usage.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1840 lines (+320 this iteration). The architect's own previous note still applies: split into routers/ — notifications.py, attendance.py, incidents.py, analytics.py, audit.py are the obvious candidates. Each of the new sections is self-contained and would lift out cleanly.

check_rate() uses a process-local defaultdict. It works for the single-container preview but will NOT enforce limits across replicas in a true prod deployment — swap for Redis (or Mongo with TTL index) before horizontal scaling. Comment in the code even says so.

GET /api/notifications/me has side-effects — it creates 'credential expiring soon' warnings on the fly. A read endpoint that writes is surprising; move the expiry scan into a background task / cron and keep /notifications/me read-only.
Incident /resolve accepts any `payload` dict. Tighten with a Pydantic model (resolution: str) so it rejects malformed bodies cleanly rather than storing {} into resolution.
Audit log stores ISO-string timestamps in 'at' — ordering works today but consider BSON datetime + an index on ('at': -1) once the collection grows.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter4.py (new — 29 tests covering health/version, per-email login rate-limit 10-in-60s, notifications CRUD + approval/rejection wiring + mark-all-read, audit log role-gating + action coverage, attendance record/me/roster, incidents report/list/resolve + role denial, analytics shape + admin-only gating, and iter<=3 regression smoke)

success_rate.frontend: All nav-scoping, bell, analytics, audit, attendance flows visually confirmed per screenshots; role-based redirect for direct /admin/* access not fully asserted due to login-redirect URL-pattern mismatch in the Playwright script — UI code uses <Protected roles=...> which is the correct pattern and ADMIN/INSTRUCTOR/STUDENT sidebars clearly differ in the captured screenshots.

test_credentials: admin@lcewai.org/Admin@LCE2026, instructor@lcewai.org/Teach@LCE2026, student@lcewai.org/Learn@LCE2026 — all working. New test identity: ratetest_<uuid>@example.com used for rate-limit test (always 401 → 429).
seed_data_creation: test_iter4 creates: ~1 attendance session (6 rows) per run, 2 incidents (1 resolved + 1 open 'denial test'), 1 pass + 1 reject lab review on student@lcewai.org (leaves 2 lab_submissions + ~2 notifications), ~11 audit entries per run (10 failed + 0 success from the fresh rate-limit email), and 2 notifications for student (approved + warning). Nothing is deleted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run everything with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Notification side-effect on GET /api/notifications/me (auto-creates expiry warnings) means the endpoint is not strictly read-only. Rate-limit is per-email not per-IP — always use unique emails during setup to avoid burning the 10/60s budget on the fixture credentials. Lab approval flow for test: pick a track=='inperson' lab, submit, then fetch submission id from GET /api/instructor/submissions, then POST review with {status:'approved'|'rejected'} (NOT 'decision'). GET /api/credentials/me returns a dict {available, earned}, not a list. The Playwright login-redirect waits on /dashboard but admin lands at /admin and instructor at /instructor — use wait_for_selector('[data-testid="sidebar"]') instead, or wait for any of (/dashboard|/admin|/instructor).
